era_context: Continuation of the early Iron Age II. Shalmaneser III remains active in Assyrian military expansion. The Western Zhou dynasty in China is approaching its traditional collapse date (771 BCE). In the Mediterranean, Greek city-states continue their development and alphabet adoption. Egypt remains fragmented under the late 21st Dynasty.
geographic_coverage_gaps: India (limited contemporary written records for this specific year)

events.title: Shalmaneser III's continued military consolidation in Mesopotamia and the Levant
events.description: Shalmaneser III continues his systematic campaigns to expand and consolidate Assyrian control. These campaigns are documented in royal inscriptions and form part of the broader Neo-Assyrian imperial project. The campaigns extract tribute and establish vassal relationships with neighboring states.

sources.name: Shalmaneser III's royal inscriptions and palace reliefs

events.certainty_note: Shalmaneser III's reign is well-attested. Specific annual attribution is approximate, based on the known pattern of his campaigns.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific events are documented for 798 BCE in surviving sources. The entry reflects the continuation of known patterns rather than a documented event. No disconfirming evidence identified.
historiographic_note: The year 798 BCE is not marked by any documented event in surviving sources. The historical record for this year is essentially a blank, filled only by inference from the broader pattern of Shalmaneser III's reign. This illustrates the sparseness of annual-level documentation even for relatively well-documented regions and periods.

graph_edges.note: Continuation of Shalmaneser III's military program
